Title: View Company Members List
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org admin user logged in; organization has at least 3 members
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as organization admin
2. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members or Company > Members
3. [ASSERT] Verify members list page loads
4. [ASSERT] Verify list shows member name and email and role and status columns
Assertions:
[DOM] Members list page loads without errors
[DOM] Table/list shows: name and email and role and status for each member
[DOM] At least 3 members visible
[FORMAT] Role labels are readable (e.g. Admin or Purchaser or Approver)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] organization members query returns member data
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Members page returns 404; empty list when members exist; missing columns in member data

Title: Search Members by Name or Email
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org admin user logged in; organization has 5+ members
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
2. [ACT] Type a member name in search input
3. [ASSERT] Verify filtered results show matching members
4. [ACT] Clear search and type a member email
5. [ASSERT] Verify filtered results show matching member
Assertions:
[DOM] Search input visible on members page
[DOM] Results filtered by name search
[DOM] Results filtered by email search
[STATE] Search works for both name and email fields
Failure_Signals: Search not filtering results; search input missing; search only works for name but not email

Title: Members List Pagination
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org admin user; organization has >10 members
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
2. [ASSERT] Verify initial page shows first batch of members
3. [SCROLL] Scroll to bottom of list
4. [ACT] Click Load More or pagination control
5. [ASSERT] Verify additional members are loaded
6. [ASSERT] Verify no duplicate members
Assertions:
[DOM] Pagination or load-more control visible when >10 members
[DOM] Additional members loaded after action
[DOM] No duplicate entries across pages
[STATE] Total visible members matches org member count
Failure_Signals: Members truncated with no pagination; duplicate members after loading more; pagination control missing

Title: Invite Member - Open Form
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
2. [ACT] Click Invite Member or Add Member button
3. [ASSERT] Verify invite form opens
4. [ASSERT] Verify form has email and first name and last name and role fields
Assertions:
[DOM] Invite member form or modal opens
[DOM] Email input field visible
[DOM] First name and last name fields visible
[DOM] Role selector (dropdown or radio) visible
Failure_Signals: Invite button missing; form does not open; required fields missing from invite form

Title: Invite Member - Submit Invitation
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org admin user; invite form open
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
2. [ACT] Click Invite Member
3. [ACT] Fill email: invite+{{timestamp}}@example.com
4. [ACT] Fill first name: Invited
5. [ACT] Fill last name: Member
6. [ACT] Select role: Purchaser
7. [ACT] Click Send Invitation or Submit
8. [ASSERT] Verify success message displayed
9. [ASSERT] Verify invited member appears in members list with Invited or Pending status
Assertions:
[DOM] Success toast or confirmation message after sending invitation
[DOM] New member appears in list with pending/invited status
[STATE] Invitation sent to specified email
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] invite member mutation succeeds
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Invitation fails with error; no success message; invited member not appearing in list; wrong status shown

Title: Invite Member - Email Notification Sent
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org admin user; invitation just submitted
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
2. [ACT] Send invitation to a test email
3. [ASSERT] Verify success confirmation
4. [ASSERT] Check that invitation notification was triggered (via API or notification log)
Assertions:
[STATE] Email invitation triggered after submission
[DOM] Success message references email being sent
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] notification or email sending event triggered
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: No email sent; invitation recorded but notification not triggered; email to wrong address

Title: Invite Member - New Member Sign-In via Invitation Link
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to the invitation acceptance link (from email)
2. [ASSERT] Verify registration or password setup form loads
3. [ACT] Set password for the new account
4. [ACT] Submit the form
5. [ASSERT] Verify new member can sign in
6. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members as the admin
7. [ASSERT] Verify member status changed from Pending to Active
Assertions:
[DOM] Invitation link leads to password setup or registration page
[STATE] New member can sign in after setting password
[DOM] Member status updated in members list to Active
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] member status updated after acceptance
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Invitation link expired or invalid; password setup fails; member status not updating to Active

Title: Edit Member Role
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org admin user; member exists with Purchaser role
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
2. [ACT] Click on a member to view details
3. [ASSERT] Verify member detail view or edit form opens
4. [ACT] Change role from Purchaser to Admin
5. [ACT] Save changes
6. [ASSERT] Verify success message
7. [ASSERT] Verify role updated in members list
Assertions:
[DOM] Member detail view shows current role
[DOM] Role dropdown allows changing role
[STATE] Role change saved successfully
[DOM] Members list reflects updated role
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] member role update mutation succeeds
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Role change not saving; no edit capability for roles; wrong role shown after update

Title: Admin vs Purchaser Access Differences
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Two accounts: org admin and org purchaser
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as org admin
2. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
3. [ASSERT] Verify admin sees Invite Member button and can edit roles
4. [ACT] Click user name in top header → click Logout in popup (BL-AUTH-007; data-testid="main-layout.top-header.account-menu.sign-out-button")
5. [NAV] Log in as org purchaser
6. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
7. [ASSERT] Verify purchaser does NOT see Invite Member button
8. [ASSERT] Verify purchaser cannot edit member roles
Assertions:
[DOM] Admin: Invite Member button visible; role editing available
[DOM] Purchaser: Invite Member button hidden; role editing disabled or hidden
[STATE] Access differences enforce role-based permissions
Failure_Signals: Purchaser can invite members (privilege escalation); purchaser can edit roles; no access difference between admin and purchaser

Title: Block Member - Member Cannot Sign In
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org admin user; active member to be blocked
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as org admin
2. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
3. [ACT] Select a member and click Block or Deactivate action
4. [ACT] Confirm blocking action
5. [ASSERT] Verify member status changes to Blocked or Inactive
6. [ACT] Click user name in top header → click Logout in popup (BL-AUTH-007; data-testid="main-layout.top-header.account-menu.sign-out-button")
7. [NAV] Attempt to sign in as the blocked member
8. [ASSERT] Verify sign-in is denied with appropriate message
Assertions:
[DOM] Member status updated to Blocked in members list
[STATE] Blocked member login attempt is rejected
[DOM] Error message shown: account blocked or deactivated
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] member block mutation succeeds
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Blocked member can still sign in; no status change visible; blocking action fails silently

Title: Unblock Member - Member Can Sign In Again
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org admin user; member currently blocked
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as org admin
2. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
3. [ACT] Select the blocked member and click Unblock or Activate
4. [ACT] Confirm unblocking
5. [ASSERT] Verify member status changes to Active
6. [ACT] Click user name in top header → click Logout in popup (BL-AUTH-007; data-testid="main-layout.top-header.account-menu.sign-out-button")
7. [NAV] Sign in as the unblocked member
8. [ASSERT] Verify sign-in succeeds
Assertions:
[DOM] Member status updated to Active in members list
[STATE] Unblocked member can sign in successfully
[STATE] Member has access to their org context
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] member unblock mutation succeeds
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Unblocked member still cannot sign in; status not updating; unblock action fails

Title: Blocked Member Status Visible in List
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org admin user; at least one blocked member exists
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as org admin
2. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
3. [ASSERT] Verify blocked member shows distinct status indicator
4. [ASSERT] Verify status label reads Blocked or Inactive
5. [ASSERT] Verify active members have Active status
Assertions:
[DOM] Blocked members have visible Blocked or Inactive status badge
[DOM] Active members have Active status badge
[FORMAT] Status indicators use distinct colors or styling
Failure_Signals: No status differentiation between active and blocked members; status column missing; same styling for all statuses

Title: Delegated Purchasing - Purchaser Order Goes to Approval
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Org with approval workflow enabled; purchaser account with order amount threshold
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as org purchaser
2. [ACT] Add products to cart exceeding approval threshold
3. [NAV] Proceed to checkout
4. [ACT] Complete checkout flow
5. [ASSERT] Verify order status shows Pending Approval (not Confirmed)
6. [ASSERT] Verify purchaser sees order in their orders list with pending status
Assertions:
[STATE] Order created with Pending Approval status
[DOM] Order status label shows Pending Approval or equivalent
[DOM] Order visible in purchaser's orders list
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] order query returns status PendingApproval
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Order auto-confirmed without approval; wrong status shown; order not visible in purchaser's list

Title: Delegated Purchasing - Approver Sees Pending Orders
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org approver account; at least one order pending approval
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as org approver
2. [NAV] Navigate to pending approvals or orders requiring approval
3. [ASSERT] Verify pending order(s) are listed
4. [ASSERT] Verify order details accessible: product and amount and purchaser name
Assertions:
[DOM] Pending approvals page/section loads
[DOM] Pending order(s) listed with order details
[DOM] Purchaser name visible on pending order
[FORMAT] Order amount displayed correctly
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] pending approvals query returns orders
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: No pending orders visible to approver; approvals page missing; order details incomplete

Title: Delegated Purchasing - Approve Order
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as org approver
2. [NAV] Navigate to pending approvals
3. [ACT] Click Approve on a pending order
4. [ACT] Confirm approval
5. [ASSERT] Verify order status changes to Approved or Processing
6. [NAV] Log in as purchaser
7. [ASSERT] Verify order status updated in purchaser's order list
Assertions:
[STATE] Order status changes from Pending Approval to Approved
[DOM] Approval success message displayed
[DOM] Purchaser sees updated order status
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] order approval mutation succeeds
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Approval action fails; order status unchanged; purchaser not notified; wrong status after approval

Title: Delegated Purchasing - Reject Order
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as org approver
2. [NAV] Navigate to pending approvals
3. [ACT] Click Reject on a pending order
4. [ACT] Optionally add rejection reason
5. [ACT] Confirm rejection
6. [ASSERT] Verify order status changes to Rejected
7. [NAV] Log in as purchaser
8. [ASSERT] Verify order shows Rejected status in purchaser's orders
Assertions:
[STATE] Order status changes to Rejected
[DOM] Rejection success message displayed
[DOM] Purchaser sees Rejected status on order
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] order rejection mutation succeeds
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Rejection action fails; order still shows Pending; purchaser not seeing rejection; rejection reason lost

Title: Delegated Purchasing - Amount Threshold Triggers Approval
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Org with approval threshold; purchaser account
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as org purchaser
2. [ACT] Add products to cart totaling BELOW threshold
3. [ACT] Complete checkout
4. [ASSERT] Verify order is confirmed immediately (no approval needed)
5. [ACT] Add products to cart totaling ABOVE threshold
6. [ACT] Complete checkout
7. [ASSERT] Verify order goes to Pending Approval
Assertions:
[STATE] Below-threshold order: confirmed immediately
[STATE] Above-threshold order: requires approval
[MATH] Threshold boundary correctly applied
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] below-threshold order status is Confirmed; above-threshold is PendingApproval
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: All orders go to approval regardless of amount; threshold not enforced; below-threshold order stuck in pending

Title: Company Members Count Display
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org admin; organization has known number of members
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as org admin
2. [NAV] Navigate to /company/members
3. [ASSERT] Verify member count is displayed
4. [ASSERT] Verify count matches actual visible members
Assertions:
[DOM] Member count shown on page (header or footer)
[MATH] Displayed count matches number of members in list
[FORMAT] Count formatted properly
Failure_Signals: Member count missing; count does not match actual members; count not updating after invite/block
